subject: Computer Networking
title: Search Engines: Finding the Web
description: A quick, practical guide to Search Engines: Finding the Web.

hook.text: The early web had no index. Finding anything meant following links from page to page. Then search engines appeared.

buildup.text: Yahoo started as a directory — humans categorized sites by hand. AltaVista used automated crawlers instead.

discovery.text: Google launched in 1998 with PageRank — ranking pages by how many other pages linked to them. Brilliant and simple.

twist.text: Google's clean interface was mocked at first. Rivals had portals crammed with news, weather, and ads.

climax.text: By 2004, Google handled most web searches. 'Google it' became a verb in everyday language.

punchline.text: Google didn't build the web. It made it findable.

quiz.question: What algorithm made Google's search results better than competitors?
options: PageRank, which ranked pages by incoming links
An AI that read every page's content
A system where users voted on page quality
